     Edit freely; this file is not regenerated. -->

# DECISION_LOG

## How to read this file

Each row is a decision the project has made. The four columns
hold *receipts*: a one- or two-sentence prose cell each. Detail
belongs in the linked build-sheet section, spec sheet, or run
artifact — not in the row itself.

The cell budget exists so the log stays a navigable receipt and
does not grow back into duplicated SPEC content. Newer rows are
expected to honor the budget; older rows may be grandfathered.

## How to add a row

1. Pick the next sequential ID (`D###`).
2. Write the four cells:
   - **Decision** — what we decided. Imperative, present tense.
   - **Context** — why we needed to decide. The tension we
     were resolving.
   - **Consequence** — what changes as a result. Cite the
     build-sheet section or run artifact.
   - **Revisit** — the conditions that would justify reopening
     this decision.
3. Cite the source (run artifact, spec sheet, or measurement).

## Decisions

| ID | Status | Decision | Context | Consequence | Revisit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| D001 | accepted | Trailer is the **FT712S2-D** (ACG FasTrac Deluxe) — 7×12 single axle, 3,500 lb GVWR, single 3.5K electric-brake axle, 2"×4" main rails, rear ramp door. **VIN 7V0W11214TU444163** (serial 444163). | Model was mis-recorded across older notes (6×10, 7×12 tandem, FT714); needed the real model + VIN to anchor dimensions and the build. | Confirmed against the [factory work order](reference/wells-cargo-ft712s2-d-work-order.md). Actual interior 81"×157", overall ~16'0"; 2"×4" rails + 4× factory 5,000 lb D-rings available for anchoring. Re-cut E-track to actual dims. | Owner to weigh the built unit for real payload; a different unit/VIN reopens. |
| D002 | proposed | **House charger recommendation = buy Victron MultiPlus-II 48/3000/35-50 120V and return the ordered 5 kW LiTime**; primary solar = **roof 3× LG455N2W-E6 in 3S through a Victron SmartSolar MPPT 250/60-Tr**. Optional ground solar = 2× LG455 in 2S through its own small 150 V-class MPPT if used. | Goal is identifying the replacement house charger; the 3500 W LiTime is **not owned yet** and ground panels are optional margin rather than a house-charger requirement. Corrected premise: Velit fits in the nose, three roof panels fit, and five panels are available total. | [Corrected 3-panel verdict](../runs/aio-adversarial-3panel/synth/VERDICT.md), [MultiPlus specs](reference/victron-multiplus-ii-48-3000-120v-specs.md), and [SmartSolar specs](reference/victron-smartsolar-mppt-250-60-tr-specs.md) supersede the original [AIO verdict](../runs/aio-adversarial/synth/VERDICT.md). **Commissioning: cap combined charge ≤100 A**, segregate roof 3S from any ground inlet, and treat ~2200 W hot sustained AC output as the design envelope. | Owner chooses a different house charger, Victron hardware unavailable, sustained AC loads require >2200 W in heat, budget/schedule forces the LiTime 3500 fallback, or shakedown energy use changes the optional ground-panel deploy plan. |
| D003 | proposed | Climate: install neither HRV nor diesel heater for July — rough-in penetrations + CO/power strings now; one measured curbside awning window; ~12 ft manual lateral-arm awning through-bolted to the top rail. | Mild July nights + under-floor-exhaust fire/weight risk make heat a cold-weather item; the awning is the binding July deliverable. | Run completed (accept-with-findings). See runs/juplaya-climate/. | Cold-weather mission, or measured window RO differs. |
| D004 | proposed | Interior: bikes side-by-side nose-forward on flush-recessed floor E-track; bed ~27" (revised from 34" — seated-headroom math; sit-test gate)/shelf ~60"; 2×4-in-E-track-socket shoring; FRP-over-liner walls (no paint), XPS behind bed only; stowable RV step. | Reconfigurable bikes-out → sleeping-deck layout in the 81"×157"×78" interior. | **Floor-row spacing/position needs rework** — agy found ~9" handlebar interference at 24" centers and that the 2×4 steel runs at the perimeter, not off-centerline. See runs/juplaya-interior/. | After the tape-measure pass (bar width + steel locations). |
| D005 | accepted | Systems: fridge **PURCHASED — Dometic CFX3 95DZ** (95 L dual-zone, 12/24 VDC compressor; supersedes the ~50–55 L recommendation). Accessories order (keyed-alike Abloy locks, Trimax boot, LandAirSea 54, Blue Sea 5026, 12V/USB-C) stands. | DC-compressor-not-Midea held; owner sized up to dual-zone by purchase. 12/24 V auto-sensing → native on the 24 V house-bus lean. | Integration handed to the design-panel run: bus assignment (24 V native, 4.6 A), interior location for the 37.9×20.9×18.6" / 66.4 lb chest (+ lid-swing clearance), ~0.8–1.3 kWh/day vs solar; keep its spot under the 110 °F ambient ceiling. [Specs](reference/dometic-cfx3-95dz-specs.md). | Energy budget or interior fit forces a rethink. |
| D006 | proposed | **Single 24 V house bus**: one Victron Orion-Tr 48/24-16A isolated (remote on/off to cabin), Blue Sea 7443 20 A 80 V-class breaker on the 48 V side, Blue Sea 5026 as the 24 V block; **48→12 V converter deleted**; tow-vehicle 12 V stays isolated. | All house loads are 24 V-native/wide-range; the lighting bus is sunk; one converter = one quiescent draw. Owner's lean confirmed unanimously by the panel. | See the build sheet (juplaya-trailer-context.md), Power section (rail map + protection schedule + thermal derate). **Conditions:** 16 A sized for current loads (~12–15 A realistic worst case); a future 24 V diesel heater reopens sizing; cabinet venting must cover converter waste heat. | Future heater, new heavy 24 V loads, or converter thermal issues. |
| D007 | proposed | **Awning = Fiamma F45s 350 (06280B01R) on owner-fabricated standoffs at the perimeter aluminum top rail**, case at/above the roofline; **≥2 fasteners into each steel tube upright per standoff** (the rail's existing bolts mark the stations but sit at the extrusion's center — a single centered bolt can't react the moment); Tie Down S + lag/deadman anchors; wound-in discipline. Wall-mount dead; flat-roof-adapter mount dead (panels own the roof width). | Wall-mount killed **by tape (2026-06-04)**: 5-1/8" measured drip-rail-to-top-edge vs the 5.35" case. Roof measured 84-7/8" × 145.5" — the landscape panel pair fits and owns the width. | See the build sheet, Climate/Awning section. **Pass/fail gates before fabrication:** rail-section 3D scan + post tube wall thickness (tap vs through-bolt); the measured roof drawing (panels+bows+AC+standoff stations); deployed-fabric-vs-door at pitch; fasten before the FRP walls close. **Required outputs: standoff count/stations over the 138" span, fastener size/grade, couple-arm spacing, documented wind+tie-down+vibration load case.** Fallback: freestanding shade. | Gates fail (→ freestanding), or the rail scan changes the standoff concept. |
| D008 | proposed | **Fridge integration**: the purchased CFX3 95DZ runs native 24 V (10 A fuse, 14 AWG) in a curbside bay immediately aft of the personnel door, E-track strapped. | 24 V-native on the D006 bus removes all conversion; bay placement keeps lid access (bikes-out) and outside reach under the awning. | See the build sheet, Systems + Interior sections. **Thermal gate (hardened — web-val): the Dometic manual requires 50 mm all-sides clearance + forced through-flow vent and forbids "closed compartments"** — an enclosed bay is non-compliant; keep it off the MultiPlus/converter plume (110 °F ceiling; dual-zone cap >86 °F); verify lid-hinge orientation; ~1.0–1.3 kWh/day in the budget. | Bay overheats in July testing, or interior rework moves the bay. |
| D009 | proposed | **Wall substrate: pull the factory OSB during the spray-foam job and re-skin in 3/8" birch ply; FRP textured panel over the birch.** 1/2" on-hand sheet → interior fixtures; 1/4" rejected (flex at 16" OC, thins the window clamp sandwich). | Owner has access to high-quality birch; 3/8 matches factory thickness so the wall sandwich geometry is unchanged; birch beats OSB on screw bite and FRP adhesion; the foam job already has the walls open — near-zero added labor. | Feeds dimension rows 12 (build-up vs window clamp range) and 19 (verify factory OSB = 3/8"). Must be ratified **before window RO cuts**. FRP trim/adhesive selection is design-freeze item 9. | Factory skin isn't 3/8", clamp range fails the new sandwich, or birch supply falls through. |
| D010 | accepted | **Flooring: Durabak-18 Outdoor Textured light grey flexible polyurethane bed-liner coating**, 4 gal ordered 2026-06-05 for June 12–15 delivery: 3 gal base floor+cove+ramp plus 1 reserve for ramp/chock/E-track wear lanes; recessed E-track stays masked and usable. | Rubber coin roll was the old lean, but common SBR coin rubber is wrong for moto fuel/oil spills and loose sheet goods trap playa dust/water against the wood floor. Raptor's acceptable light-gray 2K path had no local availability or reliable ETA, so it did not beat Durabak on schedule. | See the build sheet, Interior/Flooring section, plus the web-validation addendum and [floor-coating verdict](../runs/floor-coating-adversarial/synth/VERDICT.md). Sequence changes: floor E-track first, liner+cove before FRP, ramp included, drain to rear by raising the tongue. Wording is **fuel/oil spill-resistant, clean promptly**, not fuel-proof. | PlexCore adhesion or fuel-drip patch fails, texture is unacceptable for camp use, or Durabak delivery slips past the June 12–15 window. |

## Web-validation pass (2026-06-05)

The original seven falsifiable rows above were fact-checked against public sources, and D010 was added later as a flooring addendum — see [docs/research/build-decision-validation.md](research/build-decision-validation.md). Detail lives there and in the build-sheet gates; the receipt-level deltas:

- **D002** ✅ revised after corrected premise and owner read — the proposed house charger is now **Victron MultiPlus-II 48/3000/35-50 120V**, while roof solar moves to **3S through a Victron SmartSolar 250/60-Tr**; "never-3S" now means **never 3S into any 145/150 V-class AIO input**.
- **D006** ✅ mostly — UL-489 / 80 V / 10 kA confirmed; **make the Class-T battery-terminal main OCP explicit** and **verify the Blue Sea SKU (7443 vs 7463)**.
- **D007** 🟡 mostly — mount sound, but retract by **~10 mph wall-only** (not >20); limiting failure is arm/extrusion bending, not pull-out — don't over-constrain.
- **D008** 🔴 mixed — electrical fine; **enclosed bay violates the Dometic manual** (50 mm all-sides + no closed compartments). See hardened gate above.
- **D009** 🟡 mostly — **trapped-moisture sandwich** is the hazard; spec **exterior/marine-glue birch**; cited precedent was a failure story.
- **Windows** 🔴 mixed — clamp ring is a single **1.5" spec → fur the opening up**; cutouts confirmed (1222 = 11-5/8×21-5/8, 2015 = 19-5/8×14-5/8); door cut needs re-framing.
- **Roof** 🟡 mostly — sandwich endorsed **if dry at closure**; spec a **cold-rated/silicone** coating, not summer-cure acrylic.
- **D010 flooring** 🟡 mostly — flexible polyurethane bed-liner direction holds; claim corrected from "fuel-proof" to **fuel/oil spill-resistant, clean promptly**; adhesion and fuel-drip patches on actual PlexCore are mandatory.
